prompt: Create a MeritForge Career Media & Hiring platform that lets students and employers use a single offline-ready site to publish career resources and manage job discovery while giving operations teams rigorous governance and measurable outcomes. The Vue.js web interface provides role-based workspaces for Student Users, Employer Managers, Content Authors, Reviewers, and System Administrators. Students can browse and play short career videos, skip items, favorite content, search, and subscribe to topics; they can resume where they left off across devices, keep a private bookshelf, and add bookmarks plus highlights/annotations that are private by default and optionally shareable to a cohort. Employers can create job posts and track applications, while students can track their employment progress milestones (for example, “resume approved” to “offer accepted”). Content Authors submit articles, videos, and job announcements into a configurable review workflow with initial, secondary, and final review stages; each submission shows a clear status banner, reviewer comments, and a one-click “return for revision” action with required reason text (minimum 20 characters). Publishing supports scheduling (e.g., 03/25/2026 9:00 AM), canary release to a configurable slice of users (default 5% for 2 hours), immediate takedown with a visible retraction notice, and a traceable publishing record for every change. An Operations Dashboard surfaces trending rankings, retention and conversion KPIs, and job posting-to-application funnel metrics with permission-controlled exports to CSV for local reporting.The backend uses FastAPI to enforce business rules, validate inputs, and expose decoupled REST-style APIs consumed by the Vue.js client over encrypted local HTTPS. PostgreSQL is the system of record for users, roles, content versions, review decisions, event telemetry (play/skip/favorite/search), job posts, applications, and student progress, with all data stored locally to keep the platform fully functional without internet access. Sensitive-word and compliance checks run from an administrator-managed local dictionary; submissions receive a risk grade (Low/Medium/High) where High is automatically blocked from publishing until final approval and Medium requires at least two distinct reviewer approvals. Reading-progress conflicts are merged deterministically using “latest update wins” per content item, while preserving a revision history for annotations. Open API and webhook capabilities are supported for on-prem/intranet integrations only, with signed requests (HMAC), idempotency keys for create/update operations, rate limiting (default 120 requests/minute per user), and an async job queue with retries and dead-letter compensation for failed deliveries. Comprehensive audit logs capture who did what and when (including IP, before/after values for sensitive changes), with searchable retention set to 365 days by default and step-up confirmation (re-enter password) required for permission changes, takedowns, and account deletions. Privacy controls include consent-based visibility scopes for contact info and photos, downloadable user data exports generated locally, hard deletion within 30 days upon request (unless legally held), encryption at rest for passwords and tokens, and centrally managed keys stored on the same server with rotation every 180 days.
